projects.title: Unit 9 Project — Coordinate Plane & Integers in Action
projects.desc: Apply graphing, integers, and the coordinate plane to a real-world capstone challenge for Unit 9.

projects.title: City Builder
projects.desc: Find distance between points on the coordinate plane to build a city (6.NS.8).

projects.title: Cartesian Coordinate Quest — Reflections & Distance
projects.desc: Measure distance between points with the same x- or y-coordinate (6.NS.8).

projects.title: Coordinate Plane Explorer — Study Guide
projects.desc: Practice finding distance on the coordinate plane with worked examples (6.NS.8).

title: Distance on the Coordinate Plane
contentObjective: I can find the distance between two points on the coordinate plane using absolute value.
languageObjective: I can explain my work using the words distance, absolute value, horizontal distance, and vertical distance.

turnAndTalk.question: Two points sit on the same horizontal line: (2, 3) and (7, 3). How can you find the distance between them without counting every square?
turnAndTalk.kernel: I can find the distance by looking at the coordinates that ___.

stems.en: The y-coordinates are the same, so I only compare the ___.
stems.es: Las coordenadas y son iguales, asi que solo comparo las ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student finds the distance by subtracting the x-coordinates (7 - 2 = 5) since the points share a horizontal line.
turnAndTalk.extend: Push students to explain why we can use absolute value to find distance even if the points were at negative coordinates.
extendStems: Absolute value helps find distance because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: One point is at (-3, 1) and another is at (4, 1). How does absolute value help you find the distance across zero?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Absolute value helps me find the distance across zero because ___.

stems.en: I add the absolute values because the points are on ___ sides of zero.
stems.es: Sumo los valores absolutos porque los puntos estan en ___ lados del cero.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student adds the absolute values (3 + 4 = 7) because the points are on opposite sides of zero.
turnAndTalk.extend: Ask students to compare finding distance when both points are on the same side of zero versus opposite sides.
extendStems: When both points are on the same side of zero, I ___ instead of add.
When they are on opposite sides, I ___ because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: Why does distance on the coordinate plane always come out positive, even when the coordinates are negative?

stems.en: Distance measures how ___ apart two points are.
stems.es: La distancia mide que tan ___ estan dos puntos.

stems.en: It can never be negative because ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student explains distance is a length (how far apart) and absolute value guarantees a non-negative result.
turnAndTalk.extend: Push students to generalize a rule for finding the distance between any two points on the same horizontal or vertical line.
extendStems: A rule for same-line distance is ___.
I know whether to add or subtract by checking ___.

turnAndTalk.question: A classmate found the distance from (-2, 5) to (6, 5) by computing 6 - 2 = 4. What did they do wrong?
turnAndTalk.kernel: The mistake was ___, and the correct distance is ___.
turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student catches that the classmate ignored the negative sign; correct distance is |6| + |-2| = 8.
turnAndTalk.extend: Ask students to critique why ignoring a negative coordinate is a common distance error and how to avoid it.
extendStems: A way to avoid it is to always ___.

vocabulary.definition: How far apart two points are. It is never negative.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Qué tan separados están dos puntos. Nunca es negativo.
vocabulary.visual: The distance from -3 to 4 on a number line is |-3| + |4| = 3 + 4 = 7 units
vocabulary.cloze: How far apart two points are on the coordinate plane is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: How far a number is from zero.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Qué tan lejos está un número de cero.
vocabulary.cloze: The distance of a number from zero, used to find distance between points, is its ___.

vocabulary.definition: How far apart two points are going left or right.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Qué tan separados están dos puntos yendo a la izquierda o a la derecha.
vocabulary.visual: From (-2, 3) to (5, 3): count from -2 to 5 = 7 units across
vocabulary.cloze: The distance between two points along the x-direction is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: How far apart two points are going up or down.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Qué tan separados están dos puntos yendo hacia arriba o hacia abajo.
vocabulary.visual: From (4, -1) to (4, 6): count from -1 to 6 = 7 units up
vocabulary.cloze: The distance between two points along the y-direction is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: A grid with a line going across and a line going up that cross.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una cuadrícula con una línea horizontal y una vertical que se cruzan.
vocabulary.visual: Two number lines crossing at (0, 0), creating four quadrants
vocabulary.cloze: The grid made by the x-axis and y-axis is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: Whole numbers and their opposites, like -2, -1, 0, 1, 2.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Números enteros y sus opuestos, como -2, -1, 0, 1, 2.
vocabulary.cloze: A whole number or its opposite is an ___.

launch.narrative: Captain Vega has two treasure locations on her map: a buried chest at (-3, 2) and a hidden cave at (4, 2). Both are at the same height (y = 2) on the map. She needs to figure out how far apart they are so she knows how much rope to bring. Another pair of treasures is at (1, -3) and (1, 5) — same column, different rows. How far apart is each pair?
launch.contextImage: Treasure map coordinate grid with two pairs of points connected by dotted lines showing distances
noticePrompts: What do the two points (-3, 2) and (4, 2) have in common?
How can you count the distance between points in the same row?
How does absolute value help when one point has a negative coordinate?
wonderPrompts: What if the two points are NOT in the same row or column?
Is the distance between -3 and 4 the same as the distance between 4 and -3?

conceptIntro.heading: How do you find distance on the coordinate plane?
conceptIntro.intro: Distance is how far apart two points are, and it is never negative. When two points share a row or a column, you can find the distance using their coordinates.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: If points are on the same side of zero, subtract; if they are on opposite sides of zero, add the absolute values.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: I will find the distance from (-3, 2) to (4, 2). Both have y = 2, so they sit on the same horizontal line.
The points are on opposite sides of zero, so I add the absolute values.
I add: 3 + 4 = 7. The distance between them is 7 units.

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: Now let's find the distance from (1, -3) to (1, 5). What do these points share? The same x = 1, so it is a vertical line.
These points are on opposite sides of zero, so do we add or subtract? We add the absolute values.
|-3| = 3 and |5| = 5, so 3 + 5 = ? The distance is 8 units.

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: In the explore, plot each treasure pair like the Chest and Cave, then find the distance between them.

explore.instructions: Plot each pair of treasure locations and find the distance between them.
explore.label: Plot the points and find the distance between each pair.

discourse.prompt: How did you use coordinates and absolute value to find the distance between two points in the same row or column?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The points ___ and ___ share the same ___ -coordinate. To find the distance, I found |___| + |___| = ___, which equals ___ units.

optional.stem: The Bank is at (2, 3) and the Library is at (2, 9). How many blocks apart are they?
optional.explanation: Same x = 2, so the segment is vertical. Distance = |9 - 3| = 6 blocks.

optional.stem: The Park is at (-4, 5) and City Hall is at (3, 5). How many blocks apart are they?
optional.explanation: Same y = 5, so the segment is horizontal. The points are on opposite sides of zero, so add the absolute values: |-4| + |3| = 4 + 3 = 7 blocks.

optional.stem: The Café is at (-2, 1) and the Gym is at (-2, -4). How many blocks apart are they?
optional.explanation: Same x = -2, so the segment is vertical. The points are on opposite sides of zero: |1| + |-4| = 1 + 4 = 5 blocks.

optional.label: Match each pair of city landmarks to the number of blocks between them.

optional.instructions: Sort each block-distance problem by whether you SUBTRACT (same side of zero) or ADD absolute values (opposite sides of zero).

categories.label: Opposite sides of zero — add absolute values

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — City Block Distance Mistake

workedExample.label: Problem
workedExample.work: Find the blocks between the Park (-4, 5) and City Hall (3, 5).

workedExample.label: Student thinking
workedExample.work: Both have y = 5, so subtract the x-values: 4 - 3 = 1.

optional.correctWork: The student dropped the negative sign and used 4 instead of -4. Because the Park is at x = -4 and City Hall is at x = 3, the points sit on opposite sides of zero. Add the absolute values: |-4| + |3| = 4 + 3 = 7 blocks. (You can also write it as |3 - (-4)| = |3 + 4| = 7.) The correct distance is 7 blocks, not 1.
hints: The Park's x-coordinate is -4, not 4. Did the student use the negative sign?
Count along the grid from -4 to 3. How many blocks is that across zero?

approaching.stem: What is the distance between (-4, 3) and (2, 3)?
approaching.explanation: Both points have y = 3, so this is a horizontal distance. |2 - (-4)| = |2 + 4| = 6 units.

approaching.stem: What is the distance between (5, -2) and (5, 4)?
approaching.explanation: Both points have x = 5, so this is a vertical distance. |4 - (-2)| = |4 + 2| = 6 units.

approaching.stem: A rectangle has vertices at (1, 1), (5, 1), (5, 4), (1, 4). What is its perimeter?

approaching.label: Find the distance between each pair of points.
approaching.instructions: Both points in each row share a coordinate. Find the distance.

approaching.label: Plot -4 and 2 on the number line. Count the distance between them.

onLevel.label: Plot these pairs and find each distance: A(-5, 3) to B(2, 3), and C(4, -2) to D(4, 4)

onLevel.instructions: Find the perimeter of each rectangle using its vertex coordinates.

onLevel.label: A horizontal segment goes from (-6, 3) to (4, 3). Show the total distance as the sum of two parts: from -6 to 0, and from 0 to 4.
onLevel.questionText: What is the total distance from (-6, 3) to (4, 3)?

onLevel.stem: Two points are at (-3, -7) and (-3, 5). What is the distance between them?

extending.title: Find the Distance Error
extending.correctWork: The student subtracted 5 - 3, but the first x-coordinate is -3, not 3. The correct calculation is |5 - (-3)| = |5 + 3| = 8 units. When points span opposite sides of an axis, you must add the absolute values: |-3| + |5| = 3 + 5 = 8.
hints: The student dropped the negative sign on -3. What is the actual difference between -3 and 5?
Try counting on the number line from -3 to 5. How many units is that?

extending.prompt: A rectangle has vertices at (-4, -2), (3, -2), (3, 5), and (-4, 5). Find the length, width, perimeter, and area of the rectangle. Show your work using absolute value.
extending.sentenceFrame: The width is |___ - (___)| = ___ units. The height is |___ - (___)| = ___ units. The perimeter is 2(___ + ___) = ___ units. The area is ___ × ___ = ___ square units.

extending.label: Plot a rectangle with one vertex in each quadrant. Label the vertices and calculate the perimeter.

optionalActivity.name: City Blocks Distance Challenge
optionalActivity.intro: Help the city planner measure how many blocks apart each landmark sits on the downtown grid.

connect.scenario: A drone starts at position (-4, 0) on a grid and needs to deliver a package to (3, 0), then fly to another delivery at (3, -5). It can only fly horizontally or vertically.
connect.promptQuestion: How does the drone use coordinate distance to calculate the total flight path?
connect.prompt: The horizontal distance from (-4, 0) to (3, 0) is ___ units because ___. The vertical distance from (3, 0) to (3, -5) is ___ units because ___. The total flight path is ___ units.

exitTicket.stem: What is the distance between the points (3, -2) and (3, 5)?
exitTicket.explanation: Both points share x = 3, so this is a vertical distance. |5 - (-2)| = |5 + 2| = 7 units.
